title: Neev in-house AI platform automates routine back-office operations
description: HDFC Bank's Neev, its in-house enterprise AI platform for model access, governance and workflow integration, is shifting routine back-office and clerical tasks to automated channels, letting the bank redeploy staff toward customer-facing and management roles.
outcome: FY26 annual report shows automation absorbed routine operations work — non-supervisory headcount fell while management hiring grew ~4,800 — as the bank repositions toward a technology-led, customer-centric model.
metric: Routine back-office work automated at scale

title: SambaNova chips bring AI inference inside the bank's own data centers
description: JPMorgan picked SambaNova as inference partner in a multi-year deal, deploying SN40/SN50 RDU systems to run in-house AI models on premises for data control and auditability, part of a shift toward owning the AI stack.

title: Century Bank co-develops Abrigo's agentic lending platform
description: Santa Fe-based Century Bank collaborated with Abrigo to help build the Abrigo Agentic Platform Experience (APX), which automates document collection, data review, exception handling, and quality control across the full loan lifecycle.
outcome: Frees lending teams to spend more time advising customers and local businesses while maintaining governance and human oversight.

title: Co-founds AI Consortium; Agentic Control Plane runs 2T tokens/month
description: Scotiabank, Sun Life, TELUS and Lightworks launched a Canadian AI Consortium to jointly build AI control infrastructure; its flagship Agentic Control Plane is in production governing agents across members and processing 2+ trillion tokens per month.
outcome: 2T+ tokens/month governed in production

title: AI-driven development of core banking systems with IBM Japan and Red Hat
description: MUFG Bank, Mitsubishi UFJ Information Technology, Red Hat and IBM Japan formed a strategic partnership to apply agent-based AI (IBM's 'Bob' coding agent) across the full financial-system lifecycle — design, coding, testing, operations and maintenance — including mainframes.
outcome: Aims to shift all platforms including the mainframe domain to AI-driven development and create a model regional financial institutions can reuse.
metric: AI-driven dev across full system lifecycle

title: In-house GenAI platform 'Neev' plus microsecond fraud detection
description: HDFC Bank deployed Neev, a proprietary generative-AI platform built over 18 months by ~150-200 in-house engineers in Gurgaon to automate routine operations, alongside a custom fraud system that analyses transactions in microseconds (incl. UPI and mule detection) to cut reliance on external vendors.

title: Expanded Stripe issuing partnership powers bank-grade cards for AI agents
description: Cross River Bank expanded its card-issuing partnership with Stripe to deliver bank-grade infrastructure for agentic commerce, including restricted, single-use virtual cards that let AI agents transact without touching underlying payment credentials.
outcome: Enables AI agents to make scoped, single-use card payments while meeting card network, AML and KYC requirements.
metric: Single-use, transaction-scoped virtual cards for agents

title: BBVA completes first AI agent-initiated payment transaction with Visa
description: BBVA and Visa completed a real-world transaction initiated by an AI agent on a cardholder's behalf under Visa's Agentic Ready programme, using Visa Intelligent Commerce, tokenization, and Visa Payment Passkeys for SCA-compliant biometric authentication.
outcome: Showed AI agents can complete SCA-compliant purchases within existing European payments infrastructure while preserving cardholder consent and issuer oversight.

title: Avatar Banking: AI avatars deliver 24/7 wealth advice in OCBC WoW app
description: OCBC unveiled the OCBC WoW app with two AI avatars, Wendy and Wayne, that interact by voice or text to track portfolios in real time, deliver personalised market news and surface investment ideas from the bank's proprietary research.
outcome: Beta live with 50 Premier Private Client customers (S$1.5M+ AUM), rolling out across Singapore over 12 months then overseas; part of the 'Next Frontier' AI-digital-data strategy, alongside hiring 600 more human relationship managers.

title: FIXR agents halve P&L reconciliation time
description: Morgan Stanley's FIXR, a production agentic system with three coordinating agents, analyses nightly P&L breaks and proposes fixes with humans approving each one; per-book reconciliation fell from up to 6 hours to 2-3 hours across ~100 controllers.
outcome: ~1,500 controller hours saved per week; 6h -> 2-3h per book

title: Digital card for AI agents via Alchemy & Nuvion partnerships
description: Visa partnered with AI developer Alchemy and cross-border platform Nuvion, including a digital card designed for AI agents to transact under Visa's agentic-commerce rails.
outcome: Strong early demand for agent-ready card credentials.

title: Model-agnostic AI architecture
description: Morgan Stanley built a model-agnostic architecture that routes each task to the best-fit model — Claude, GPT or open-source.
outcome: Best-model-per-task routing.

description: Santander's AI Lab released 12 open-source projects on GitHub, including LLM provider-switching software and a synthetic fraud-data generator.
